iconographic_features: Single horn, tasseled collar, ritual object (post/filter) in front. Bovine body.
vps2024_interpretation: Emblem of the Market Common (central commercial plaza).
parpola_interpretation: Proto-Dravidian: ekashringa (one-horned), possibly a mythical animal or clan totem.
notes: Most frequent motif. ~60% of all iconographic seals.

iconographic_features: Pronounced humped back (Bos indicus), dewlap, short curved horns.
parpola_interpretation: Likely represents the clan or village totem.

iconographic_features: Asian elephant (Elephas maximus), trunk lowered, tusks visible.
vps2024_interpretation: Elephant Street: associated with sesame oil and ghee sheds.
parpola_interpretation: Elephant may encode Proto-Dravidian word for elephant.

iconographic_features: Bull without prominent hump; shorter horns than zebu.
vps2024_interpretation: Bull Street: milk, housing for oxen, market banners.
High-value district marker; high-value cattle sheds.

iconographic_features: Long slender snout, aquatic reptile (Gavialis gangeticus).
vps2024_interpretation: Riverine district: laws governing river facility usage.
